page: Microsoft Azure D16as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any graphics processing units.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a balanced performance profile. The single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, is strong, placing in the top 25% of tested servers, while multi-core CPU performance falls within the average range. Memory subsystem testing reveals average read-only bandwidth for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in memory write operations, where large-block uncached write bandwidth is classified as weak, placing in the bottom 25%. In terms of specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models, though performance scales down to average levels for medium-sized models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16as_v5 offers a standard general-purpose resource density, balancing compute and memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or specialized accelerators. This makes it a predictable option for workloads that rely on external block storage. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average multi-core capabilities, this server is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate text processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no bundled local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small language models, while multi-core CPU performance, memory read bandwidth, and web serving throughput remain average. A key tradeoff is its weak large-block memory write bandwidth, which falls into the bottom 25%. Lacking a GPU, this instance is designed for non-accelerated workloads. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this server suitable for general-purpose applications, including web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced compute and memory performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
